evidence: The module header states 'This is an "eventually" formulation of the Second Hardy–Littlewood conjecture' and erdosproblems.com/855 identifies the problem as the second Hardy-Littlewood conjecture; the decl states the eventual form pi(x+y) <= pi(x)+pi(y).
verify_note: Decl is explicitly the "eventually" variant (∀ᶠ x ∀ᶠ y), not the Second Hardy-Littlewood conjecture itself (all x,y>=2, expected false); match_kind exact is wrong; the exact formalization target is the imported Wikipedia.HardyLittlewood decl.

evidence: The statement is an inequality pi(x+y) <= pi(x) + pi(y) about the prime-counting function pi.
verify_note: Decl exists (research open, AMS 11); QID live, label matches; pi is the sole object of the inequality.

evidence: The problem ('weak sunflower problem' per erdosproblems.com/857) asks for the minimal family size forcing a k-sunflower (IsSunflower) among subsets of [n].
verify_note: Decl exists (research open, AMS 5); QID live, label matches; IsSunflower is the defining condition of m(n,k).

evidence: The conjecture asserts the density d_t of DivisorSumSet t (via Set.HasDensity) is asymptotically c1/log(t)^c2.
verify_note: Decl exists (research open, AMS 11); QID live; Set.HasDensity is the conjectured quantity.

evidence: DivisorSumSet t is the set of n such that t is a sum of distinct divisors of n (Nat.divisors).
verify_note: Decl exists; QID live; DivisorSumSet is built from Nat.divisors, load-bearing.

evidence: Erdos's proved weaker version bounds the density d_t of DivisorSumSet t between 1/log(t)^c3 and 1/log(t)^c4.
verify_note: Decl exists (research solved, AMS 11); QID live; the bounded quantity is the density d_t.

evidence: Asks whether an additive basis of order 2 with representation function tending to infinity must contain a minimal additive basis of order 2 (IsAsymptoticAddBasisOfOrder).
verify_note: Decl exists (research solved, AMS 5 11); QID live, label matches; IsAsymptoticAddBasisOfOrder is the core notion.

evidence: Asks whether f(n) > epsilon log n forces an additive basis of order 2 to contain a minimal additive basis; Larsen and Larsen constructed a counterexample.
verify_note: Decl exists (research solved); QID live; additive basis of order 2 is the core notion.

evidence: Erdos and Nathanson proved the minimal-additive-basis containment when f(n) > (log 4/3)^-1 log n for all large n.
verify_note: Decl exists with Unicode epsilon (research solved); QID live; minimal additive basis containment is the statement.

evidence: Hartter and Nathanson proved there exist additive bases which do not contain any minimal additive bases.
verify_note: Decl exists (research solved); QID live; existence of bases without minimal sub-bases.

evidence: Erdos-Nathanson question whether an additive basis of order 2 with 1_A*1_A(n) -> infinity can be partitioned into two disjoint additive bases of order 2; disproved (Lean) by Larsen.
verify_note: Decl exists (research solved, formal_proof attr); QID live; partitioning an order-2 additive basis is the whole question (basis condition spelled out inline).

evidence: The forum variant asks whether every maximal primitive subset of {2,...,n} has at least pi(n) elements, the size of the prime maximal primitive set.
verify_note: Decl exists (research open, AMS 5 11 91); QID live; ((Icc 2 n).filter Nat.Prime).card is pi(n), the load-bearing lower bound.

evidence: F(A,X,k) counts indices i with [a_i,...,a_{i+k-1}] < X where the bracket is the least common multiple (Finset.range k).lcm.
verify_note: Decl exists (research open, AMS 11); QID live, label matches; Finset.lcm is the bracket the count is defined by.

evidence: Asks whether removing a suitable infinite subset from a minimal additive basis of order k leaves an additive basis of order k+1 (IsAsymptoticAddBasisOfOrder).
verify_note: Decl exists (research open, AMS 5 11); QID live; minimal asymptotic additive basis is the central notion.
